TDM Warp Specialization

Narrowed warp mapping for TDM copies in Triton AMDGPU

Jungwook Park
Early design exploration

Motivation

In warp-pipelined kernels on gfx1250, all 8 warps issue identical TDM copies. But global memory latency is the same regardless of how many warps request it.

Key observation: If only the upper warp group (warps 0–3, one stage ahead) issues TDM copies, the memory request is invoked earlier relative to when the lower group needs the data.

  • The phase shift provides natural latency tolerance
  • By the time the lower group needs data, it's already in LDS
  • Goal: improve latency hiding without changing the programming model

Background: Warp-Pipeline Groups

8 warps split into two groups by threadIdx.x:

warpIDX = threadIdx.x / (warpSize × 4)
warpLow  = (warpIDX == 0) → warps {0,1,2,3}
warpHigh = (warpIDX != 0) → warps {4,5,6,7}

Round-robin SIMD assignment → each group has one warp per SIMD.

warpLow enters the loop first (one stage ahead).

SIMDwarpLowwarpHigh
0warp 0warp 4
1warp 1warp 5
2warp 2warp 6
3warp 3warp 7

Background: TDM Copy ↔ async_wait

Hardware coupling

  • tdm_copy — async global→LDS transfer for this warp's sub-tile
  • async_wait(N) — stall until ≤N of this warp's copies remain
  • Warp 0 cannot wait on warp 4's copies

Count-based, not handle-based

  • No token or tag to bind a wait to a specific copy
  • Association is implicit via FIFO ordering
tdm_copy(A)      # outstanding = 1
tdm_copy(B)      # outstanding = 2
tdm_copy(scaleA) # outstanding = 3
tdm_copy(scaleB) # outstanding = 4

async_wait(2)    # ≤2 remain → A,B done
... use A, B ...

async_wait(0)    # 0 remain → all done
... use scaleA, scaleB ...

Three Challenges

These hazards prevented simpler alternatives and shaped the final design.

Challenge 1: Co-predication

tdm_copy and async_wait must run on the same warps. But async_wait sits between stages — it can't inherit a stage's annotation.

Challenge 2: Count-based copy-to-wait association

Multiple copies + multiple waits → compiler can't match them individually. Splitting copies across warp groups breaks wait counts.

Resolved Epoch-based all-or-nothing analysis using async_wait(0) boundaries.

Challenge 3: Descriptor construction separation

make_tensor_desc and tdm_copy are separate ops. Annotating the copy doesn't change the descriptor's sub-tile layout.

Resolved Place tdm_specialize=True on the descriptor, not the copy.

Solution: Narrowed Warp Mapping

Only the first 4 warps are active for TDM. The block is remapped so they cover the full tile.

Default (8 warps)

warp 0 → [32, 64]
warp 1 → [32, 64]
warp 2 → [32, 64]
warp 3 → [32, 64]
warp 4 → [32, 64]
warp 5 → [32, 64]
warp 6 → [32, 64]
warp 7 → [32, 64]

Specialized (4 warps)

warp 0 → [64, 64] ✓
warp 1 → [64, 64] ✓
warp 2 → [64, 64] ✓
warp 3 → [64, 64] ✓
warp 4 → inactive
warp 5 → inactive
warp 6 → inactive
warp 7 → inactive

Full block [256, 64] copied to LDS. Content identical. Only work distribution changes.

Constraint & Compiler Actions

Hard constraint: no mixing within an epoch

Specialized and non-specialized TDM copies cannot coexist between two async_wait(0) boundaries. Mixing breaks FIFO count semantics — the compiler emits an error.

Compiler actions

#Action
1Build TDM descriptor with numWarps=4
2Predicate tdm_copy — only warps 0–3 execute
3Predicate async_wait — only warps 0–3 wait
4Insert barrier after wait — warps 4–7 see updated LDS
5Verify no mixing within any epoch

Wait counts unchanged — each active warp issues the same number of copies, just with larger sub-tiles.

Triggering: tdm_specialize on Descriptor

Boolean on make_tensor_desc. Explicit, self-contained, not tied to warp-pipelining.

Gluon API

desc_a = tdm.make_tensor_desc(
    ..., tdm_specialize=True)
desc_b = tdm.make_tensor_desc(
    ..., tdm_specialize=True)

for k in range(0, K, BLOCK_K):
    tdm.async_wait(N)        # predicated
    with warp_pipeline_stage("memory"):
        tdm.copy(desc_a, buf_a) # predicated
        tdm.copy(desc_b, buf_b) # predicated
    with warp_pipeline_stage("compute"):
        acc = wmma(a, b, acc)   # all warps

IR

module attributes {
  ttg.num-warps = 8
}

%desc_a = tt.make_tensor_desc ...
           { tdm.specialize = true }

%desc_b = tt.make_tensor_desc ...
           // no attr → default

Also works standalone

Without warp-pipelining, compiler predicates with warp_id < 4 and inserts s_barrier.

Epoch-Based Analysis

async_wait(0) = full FIFO drain → natural epoch boundary for local reasoning.

       ┌─── epoch 1 ──────────────────────┐
       │ tdm_copy(desc_spec_A) ← specialize│
       │ tdm_copy(desc_spec_B) ← specialize│
       │ async_wait(2)         ← predicate │
       │ ... use A ...                     │
       │ async_wait(0)  ──── DRAIN ────────┘  ← boundary
       │
       ├─── epoch 2 ──────────────────────┐
       │ tdm_copy(desc_full_C) ← full     │
       │ async_wait(0)  ──── DRAIN ────────┘  ← leave alone
       │
       ├─── epoch 3 ──────────────────────┐
       │ tdm_copy(desc_spec_D) ← specialize│
       │ async_wait(0)  ──── DRAIN ────────┘  ← predicate
All specialized? → Predicate entire epoch.
None specialized? → Leave untouched.
Mixed? → Error.

Phase-Shift Synergy

The warp-pipeline phase shift is what makes this optimization most effective.

Phase 0:  Upper(warpLow) → tdm_copy(tile[1])          Lower(warpHigh) → (deferred)
Phase 1:  Upper           → async_wait; mfma(tile[0])   Lower → mfma(tile[0])
Phase 2:  Upper           → tdm_copy(tile[2])          Lower → mfma(tile[1])

Data flow for tile[1]:

  • Upper issues tdm_copy(tile[1]) at Phase 0
  • Upper does async_wait at Phase 1 — data is in LDS
  • Lower needs tile[1] at Phase 2 — one full phase after data is ready
Upper groupLower group
Currentasync_wait between stagesasync_wait between stages
Specializedasync_wait between stagesnothing — CondBarrier covers it

Alternative Triggering Mechanisms

All share the same challenges. Epoch analysis resolves Challenge 2 for all. The differentiator is descriptor construction (Challenge 3) and API design.

OptionApproachVerdict
A Stage attribute + paired copies (each warp covers partner's sub-tile) Viable but doubles FIFO, adjusts wait counts, tied to pipelining
B (stride, count) on tdm_copy ops Insufficient doesn't affect descriptor (Challenge 3)
C warpsPerCTA on TDM ops Insufficient conflates layout vs module concepts + Challenge 3
D Per-loop attribute (tdm_warp_specialize() directive) Viable but compiler must still rebuild descriptors internally (Challenge 3)
E Compiler flag only Escape hatch too coarse alone, good supplement
F Auto-detection from IR patterns Supplement can't determine profitability
G Warp-level programming (warp_group context manager) Undesirable breaks block-level SPMD model

Summary

AspectDesign
Signaltdm_specialize=True on make_tensor_desc
APIBoolean — simple, no warp count math
IRtdm.specialize = true attribute on descriptor op
DescriptorBuilt with numWarps=4 (first 4 warps)
AnalysisEpoch-based: split at async_wait(0), all-or-nothing per epoch
PredicationwarpLow (pipelined) or warp_id < 4 (standalone)
Wait countsUnchanged — same FIFO per active warp
Phase synergyLower group gets free latency hiding from deferral
ScopeNot tied to warp-pipelining — works standalone
Escape hatch-amdgpu-tdm-warp-specialize={off|on|auto}
← → arrow keys  |  click to advance
